subject: ap-government
title: Concept Application — Taylor Swift, Voter Registration, and Youth Turnout
stimulus:
In October of 2018, musician Taylor Swift used the social media site Instagram to endorse candidates for the United States Congress in the upcoming election. The post was a rare political statement from the pop star, who noted that "I've been reluctant to publicly voice my political opinions, but I feel very differently about that now. I always have and always will cast my vote based on which candidate will protect and fight for the human rights I believe we all deserve in this country.... So many intelligent, thoughtful, self-possessed people have turned 18 in the past two years and now have the right and privilege to make their vote count. But first you need to register, which is quick and easy to do."

Immediately following Swift's Instagram post, thousands of 18–24 year olds signed up to vote. Swift added a link to the website vote.org in her bio, which lists voting procedures in various states.

parts.prompt: Referencing the scenario, describe the structural barrier to voting that is discussed above.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: Describes voter registration procedures as the structural barrier to voting referenced in the scenario — Taylor Swift's post addresses registration requirements, which must be completed before voting.
parts.scoring_notes: The response must identify voter registration as the structural barrier referenced in the scenario. Accept any description that correctly identifies registration requirements as the barrier.

parts.prompt: Explain why the structural barrier described in part A may be more of an obstacle in some states than in others.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: Explains that voter registration requirements vary by state because states control election rules under federalism. Accept: registration procedures vary from state to state due to federalism; legislators and citizens in different states have different views on what registration procedures should be in place; some states have same-day registration while others have earlier deadlines.
parts.scoring_notes: The response must connect variation in the structural barrier to federalism or state-controlled election rules. Generic statements about states being different do not earn the point.

parts.prompt: If youth voter turnout increases as a result of the actions in the scenario, explain how this could affect the way candidates run campaigns in the future.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: Explains a specific effect on campaigning. Accept: candidates might recruit celebrities to campaign on social media; candidates may engage supporters on social media to register, vote, donate, or attend events; candidates may adopt issues more important to younger voters.
parts.scoring_notes: The response must explain a specific change in campaigning strategy driven by increased youth voter turnout. Accept any plausible campaign adjustment that addresses the youth demographic.
